# Feature: Protocol Selector & Conditional Editor Shell

## 1. Feature Goal

**ID**: FEAT-003  
**Source Plan**: `.spec-lite/plan_websocket_sse.md`

Make the request editor protocol-aware so it morphs its layout, controls, and tab structure to match the selected protocol (HTTP, WS, or SSE). This delivers the visual scaffolding that all subsequent realtime UI features (FEAT-006, FEAT-007) will build on. Users can switch any request tab to WebSocket or SSE via a new `ProtocolSelector` dropdown in the toolbar. The editor immediately reconfigures: the HTTP method dropdown, the Send button, request section tabs, and the response output area all change to reflect the protocol's interaction model. All changes leave the HTTP path 100% unmodified.

## 2. Data Model (Granular)

No new persisted entities. This feature extends the existing type model already defined in FEAT-001 and FEAT-002.

### Changes to `ResponseSectionTab` (in `packages/core/src/types/tab.ts`)

The current union:
```ts
export type ResponseSectionTab = 'Error' | 'Body' | 'Headers' | 'Validation';
```
Must be extended to:
```ts
export type ResponseSectionTab = 'Error' | 'Body' | 'Headers' | 'Validation' | 'Messages' | 'Events';
```

| New value    | Used by   | Purpose                                                  |
|-------------|-----------|----------------------------------------------------------|
| `'Messages'` | WS tabs   | WebSocket message timeline (timeline content in FEAT-006)|
| `'Events'`   | SSE tabs  | SSE event stream (stream content in FEAT-007)            |

### New helpers added to `packages/core/src/types/tab.ts`

```ts
/** Returns the valid request-side section tabs for `protocol`. */
export function getRequestTabsForProtocol(protocol: RequestProtocol): RequestSectionTab[];

/** Returns the valid response-side output tabs for `protocol`. */
export function getResponseTabsForProtocol(protocol: RequestProtocol): ResponseSectionTab[];
```

| Protocol | Request tabs                              | Response tabs                 |
|----------|-------------------------------------------|-------------------------------|
| `'http'` | `Params`, `Headers`, `Body`, `Validation` | `Body`, `Headers`, `Validation` (+ `Error` conditionally) |
| `'ws'`   | `Params`, `Headers`                       | `Messages`, `Headers`         |
| `'sse'`  | `Params`, `Headers`, `Body`               | `Events`, `Headers`           |

### Protocol-aware section defaults (updated)

`getDefaultResponseSection(protocol)` currently returns `'Body'` for all protocols (the codebase comments explicitly note this will be fixed in FEAT-003):
- `'http'` → `'Body'` (unchanged)
- `'ws'`   → `'Messages'` (changed from `'Body'`)
- `'sse'`  → `'Events'` (changed from `'Body'`)

### New UI components (Core)

| Component              | Location                                             | Purpose                                               |
|------------------------|------------------------------------------------------|-------------------------------------------------------|
| `ProtocolSelector`     | `packages/core/src/components/common/`               | Protocol dropdown (HTTP/WS/SSE) for the editor toolbar |
| `ConnectionControls`   | `packages/core/src/components/common/`               | Status indicator + Connect/Disconnect toggle button   |
| `WsOutputArea`         | `packages/core/src/components/common/`               | WS tab output area shell: Messages + Response Headers  |
| `SseOutputArea`        | `packages/core/src/components/common/`               | SSE tab output area shell: Events + Response Headers   |

All four components are platform-agnostic, live in `packages/core`, and are consumed by both platform `RequestEditor` implementations.

### Relationships

- `TabData.protocol` (1:1) drives which toolbar controls, request tabs, and output area are rendered in `RequestEditor`.
- `RealtimeTabState.status` (read-only, via `useAppStateStore`) drives `ConnectionControls` button state and dot color.
- `RealtimeTabState.responseHeaders` (read-only) is displayed in the "Response Headers" pane inside `WsOutputArea` and `SseOutputArea`.

## 3. Files

### Created

- `packages/core/src/components/common/ProtocolSelector.tsx` — Protocol dropdown component with Globe/Cable/Radio icons.
- `packages/core/src/components/common/ConnectionControls.tsx` — Status dot + label + Connect/Disconnect button.
- `packages/core/src/components/common/WsOutputArea.tsx` — WS output shell: `[Messages] [Response Headers]` tabs with empty state placeholders in Messages; headers pane reads from realtime state.
- `packages/core/src/components/common/SseOutputArea.tsx` — SSE output shell: `[Events] [Response Headers]` tabs with empty state placeholders in Events; headers pane reads from realtime state.
- `packages/core/src/test/components/common/ProtocolSelector.test.tsx` — Component tests.
- `packages/core/src/test/components/common/ConnectionControls.test.tsx` — Component tests.
- `packages/core/src/test/components/common/WsOutputArea.test.tsx` — Shell component tests.
- `packages/core/src/test/components/common/SseOutputArea.test.tsx` — Shell component tests.

### Modified

- `packages/core/src/types/tab.ts` — Extend `ResponseSectionTab`, update `getDefaultResponseSection`, add `getRequestTabsForProtocol` and `getResponseTabsForProtocol`.
- `packages/core/src/components/common/TabsBar.tsx` — Show `WS` / `SSE` protocol badge instead of HTTP method badge for non-HTTP tabs.
- `packages/core/src/components/common/index.ts` — Export four new components.
- `packages/core/src/index.ts` — Re-export four new components and two new helpers.
- `packages/vscode/src/webview/RequestEditor.tsx` — Protocol-conditional toolbar, request tabs, and output area.
- `packages/web/src/components/RequestEditor.tsx` — Same changes as above (kept in sync).

## 4. Dependencies

- **FEAT-001** (Protocol Types & Core Data Model) — `AnyCollectionRequest`, `RequestProtocol`, `WsCollectionRequest`, `SseCollectionRequest` — **Complete**.
- **FEAT-002** (Realtime State Slice) — `RealtimeTabState`, `ConnectionStatus`, `RealtimeSlice` actions — **Complete**.
- Existing `useAppStateStore` (`updateProtocol`, `getRealtimeState`, `setActiveRequestSection`, `setActiveResponseSection`) — all in place.

## 5. Implementation Tasks

### TASK-001: Extend `ResponseSectionTab` and add protocol-aware tab helpers in `tab.ts`

- [ ] **Implementation**:
  - In `packages/core/src/types/tab.ts`:
    - Extend `ResponseSectionTab` union with `'Messages'` and `'Events'`.
    - Update `getDefaultResponseSection(protocol)` to return:
      - `'http'` → `'Body'`
      - `'ws'`  → `'Messages'`
      - `'sse'` → `'Events'`
    - Add `getRequestTabsForProtocol(protocol: RequestProtocol): RequestSectionTab[]`:
      - `'http'` → `['Params', 'Headers', 'Body', 'Validation']`
      - `'ws'`   → `['Params', 'Headers']`
      - `'sse'`  → `['Params', 'Headers', 'Body']`
    - Add `getResponseTabsForProtocol(protocol: RequestProtocol): ResponseSectionTab[]`:
      - `'http'` → `['Body', 'Headers', 'Validation']`
      - `'ws'`   → `['Messages', 'Headers']`
      - `'sse'`  → `['Events', 'Headers']`
  - Export the two new helpers from `packages/core/src/index.ts`.

- [ ] **Unit Tests** (`packages/core/src/test/types/tab.test.ts` — new file or extend existing):
  - `getRequestTabsForProtocol('http')` returns all 4 HTTP sections in order.
  - `getRequestTabsForProtocol('ws')` returns only `['Params', 'Headers']`.
  - `getRequestTabsForProtocol('sse')` returns `['Params', 'Headers', 'Body']`.
  - `getResponseTabsForProtocol('http')` returns `['Body', 'Headers', 'Validation']`.
  - `getResponseTabsForProtocol('ws')` returns `['Messages', 'Headers']`.
  - `getResponseTabsForProtocol('sse')` returns `['Events', 'Headers']`.
  - `getDefaultResponseSection('http')` returns `'Body'`.
  - `getDefaultResponseSection('ws')` returns `'Messages'`.
  - `getDefaultResponseSection('sse')` returns `'Events'`.

- [ ] **Documentation Update**: Update JSDoc on `getDefaultResponseSection` to remove the FEAT-003 stub note. Add JSDoc to the two new helper functions.

- **Verify**: Run `pnpm --filter @wave-client/core test`. All new and existing tests pass with no TypeScript errors.

### TASK-002: `ProtocolSelector` core component

- [ ] **Implementation**:
  - Create `packages/core/src/components/common/ProtocolSelector.tsx`.
  - The component is a styled dropdown (Radix `Select`) with three options:
    - HTTP: `GlobeIcon` + label "HTTP" — accent color `text-blue-600 dark:text-blue-400`
    - WS: `CableIcon` + label "WebSocket" — accent color `text-teal-600 dark:text-teal-400`
    - SSE: `RadioIcon` + label "SSE" — accent color `text-purple-600 dark:text-purple-400`
  - The component accepts no props; it reads `activeTab.protocol` from `useAppStateStore` and calls `updateProtocol` on selection.
  - If `activeTab` is null (no open tab), render `null`.
  - Styling: consistent with the Method dropdown in `RequestEditor` — same width/height and dark-mode styled border.
  - Export from `packages/core/src/components/common/index.ts`.
  - Export from `packages/core/src/index.ts`.

- [ ] **Unit Tests** (`packages/core/src/test/components/common/ProtocolSelector.test.tsx`):
  - Renders without crashing with an HTTP tab active.
  - Shows "HTTP" as the active selection when `protocol === 'http'`.
  - Shows "WebSocket" as the active selection when `protocol === 'ws'`.
  - Shows "SSE" as the active selection when `protocol === 'sse'`.
  - Calls `updateProtocol('ws')` when the user selects the WebSocket option.
  - Calls `updateProtocol('sse')` when the user selects the SSE option.
  - Calls `updateProtocol('http')` when the user selects the HTTP option.
  - Renders `null` when there is no active tab.
  - Use `createMockAdapter` + `AdapterProvider` + a seeded Zustand store (via `useAppStateStore` preloaded state) to set up tab state.

- [ ] **Documentation Update**: Add component-level JSDoc explaining purpose and design constraints.

- **Verify**: Component renders correctly in all three protocol states and selection triggers correct store action.

### TASK-003: `ConnectionControls` core component

- [ ] **Implementation**:
  - Create `packages/core/src/components/common/ConnectionControls.tsx`.
  - Props interface:
    ```ts
    interface ConnectionControlsProps {
      tabId: string;
      onConnect: () => void;
      onDisconnect: () => void;
      disabled?: boolean;
    }
    ```
  - Reads `realtimeState` for `tabId` via `useAppStateStore((s) => s.getRealtimeState(tabId))`.
  - **Status indicator**: A small filled circle whose color maps to `ConnectionStatus`:
    - `idle` → `text-slate-400` (grey)
    - `connecting` → `text-yellow-500` (yellow, optionally pulsing)
    - `connected` → `text-green-500` (green)
    - `disconnecting` → `text-yellow-500` (yellow)
    - `disconnected` → `text-slate-500` (darker grey)
    - `error` → `text-red-500` (red)
  - **Status label** (short text next to the dot): `Idle`, `Connecting…`, `Connected`, `Disconnecting…`, `Disconnected`, `Error`.
  - **Action button**: A single `PrimaryButton`:
    - Shows **"Connect"** when status is `idle`, `disconnected`, or `error`.
    - Shows **"Disconnect"** when status is `connecting`, `connected`, or `disconnecting`.
    - Disabled when `disabled` prop is `true` OR when status is `connecting` or `disconnecting` (transitional states).
    - Calls `onConnect` or `onDisconnect` as appropriate.
  - Export from `packages/core/src/components/common/index.ts` and `packages/core/src/index.ts`.

- [ ] **Unit Tests** (`packages/core/src/test/components/common/ConnectionControls.test.tsx`):
  - Shows "Connect" button and "Idle" label when status is `idle`.
  - Shows "Connect" button when status is `disconnected`.
  - Shows "Connect" button when status is `error`.
  - Shows "Disconnect" button and "Connected" label when status is `connected`.
  - Shows "Disconnect" button and "Connecting…" label when status is `connecting`.
  - Connect button is disabled when `disabled={true}`.
  - Connect button is disabled when status is `connecting`.
  - Connect button is disabled when status is `disconnecting`.
  - Clicking Connect calls `onConnect`.
  - Clicking Disconnect calls `onDisconnect`.
  - Status dot has correct color class for each status variant (assert on ARIA label or test-id if needed).
  - Use `createMockAdapter` + pre-seeded Zustand store to set up realtime state per test.

- [ ] **Documentation Update**: JSDoc on props and status color logic.

- **Verify**: All status states render the correct button and label. Button calls appropriate handler.

### TASK-004: `WsOutputArea` and `SseOutputArea` shell components

- [ ] **Implementation** (`packages/core/src/components/common/`):

  **`WsOutputArea.tsx`**:
  - Props: `tabId: string`.
  - Renders two tabs: **Messages** and **Response Headers** (using the same tab button pattern as `RequestEditor`'s existing request/response section tabs — `border-b-2 border-blue-500` for active).
  - Manages local state `activeTab: 'Messages' | 'Headers'` (default `'Messages'`).
  - **Messages tab content (FEAT-003 stub)**: Empty state placeholder with `MessageSquareIcon` (Lucide), bold "No messages yet" heading, and subtext "Connect to a WebSocket endpoint to see messages here." This entire placeholder will be replaced in FEAT-006.
  - **Response Headers tab content**: If `realtimeState.responseHeaders` (from `useAppStateStore`) is non-empty, renders a key/value list identical to HTTP `responseData.headers` rendering style in `RequestEditor`. If empty, shows a short placeholder: "Response headers will appear here after connecting."
  - Export from `packages/core/src/components/common/index.ts` and `packages/core/src/index.ts`.

  **`SseOutputArea.tsx`**:
  - Props: `tabId: string`.
  - Renders two tabs: **Events** and **Response Headers** (same pattern).
  - Manages local state `activeTab: 'Events' | 'Headers'` (default `'Events'`).
  - **Events tab content (FEAT-003 stub)**: Empty state placeholder with `RadioIcon` (Lucide), bold "No events yet" heading, and subtext "Connect to an SSE endpoint to see events here." This entire placeholder will be replaced in FEAT-007.
  - **Response Headers tab content**: Same as `WsOutputArea` — read `realtimeState.responseHeaders` from store.
  - Export from `packages/core/src/components/common/index.ts` and `packages/core/src/index.ts`.

- [ ] **Unit Tests**:
  - `packages/core/src/test/components/common/WsOutputArea.test.tsx`:
    - Shows "No messages yet" placeholder when `wsMessages` is empty and status is not connected.
    - Clicking "Response Headers" tab switches to headers view.
    - Shows key/value pairs when `realtimeState.responseHeaders` is non-empty.
    - Shows headers placeholder when `responseHeaders` is empty.
  - `packages/core/src/test/components/common/SseOutputArea.test.tsx`:
    - Shows "No events yet" placeholder when `sseEvents` is empty.
    - Clicking "Response Headers" tab switches to headers view.
    - Shows key/value pairs when `realtimeState.responseHeaders` is non-empty.
    - Shows headers placeholder when `responseHeaders` is empty.

- [ ] **Documentation Update**: JSDoc on each component and its stub intent (references FEAT-006/FEAT-007 for full implementation).

- **Verify**: Both components render their default empty state and tab switching works correctly.

### TASK-005: `TabsBar` protocol badge for WS and SSE tabs

- [ ] **Implementation**:
  - Modify `packages/core/src/components/common/TabsBar.tsx`.
  - The existing "Method Badge" reads `tab.method?.substring(0, 3) || 'GET'` and colors it via `getMethodColor(tab.method)`.
  - Change the rendering logic:
    - If `tab.protocol === 'ws'`: render `WS` badge with `text-teal-600 dark:text-teal-400` and a short underline or `font-bold` styling (no method concept for WS).
    - If `tab.protocol === 'sse'`: render `SSE` badge with `text-purple-600 dark:text-purple-400`.
    - Otherwise (HTTP or undefined): existing `getMethodColor(tab.method)` / `tab.method?.substring(0,3)` logic unchanged.
  - Requires importing `tab.protocol` (it is already part of `TabData`, so no new imports needed — `tab` is the full `TabData` object).

- [ ] **Unit Tests** (`packages/core/src/test/components/common/TabsBar.test.tsx` — create if it does not exist):
  - HTTP tab shows `"GET"` (or current method) in the method-color style.
  - WS tab shows `"WS"` badge with teal styling, not the HTTP method.
  - SSE tab shows `"SSE"` badge with purple styling.
  - Protocol badge does not interfere with tab click, close, or dirty-indicator behavior.
  - All existing `TabsBar` behaviors (add tab button, close confirmation, keyboard shortcuts Ctrl+T / Ctrl+W) continue to function.

- [ ] **Documentation Update**: Update the comment block on the "Method Badge" section to note the protocol-aware rendering.

- **Verify**: Open three tabs where one is HTTP/GET, one is WS, and one is SSE. Each tab badge shows the correct label with the correct color.

### TASK-006: `RequestEditor` protocol-conditional toolbar, request tabs, and output area

> This task modifies both `packages/vscode/src/webview/RequestEditor.tsx` and `packages/web/src/components/RequestEditor.tsx`. The logic is identical in both files — maintain parity.

- [ ] **Implementation**:

  **Toolbar changes (per-protocol)**:
  - Import `ProtocolSelector` and `ConnectionControls` from `@wave-client/core`.
  - Add `ProtocolSelector` to the "Top Bar" (`{/* Top Bar - Method, URL, Send */}`) as a trailing element (after the Send / Connection Controls button).
  - Apply conditional rendering based on `activeTab.protocol`:
    - **HTTP** (`protocol === 'http'` or `undefined`): render exactly the existing method dropdown + Send button. No changes to this branch.
    - **WS** (`protocol === 'ws'`): hide the method dropdown; replace the Send button with `<ConnectionControls tabId={activeTabId} onConnect={() => {/* TODO FEAT-006 */}} onDisconnect={() => {/* TODO FEAT-006 */}} />`.
    - **SSE** (`protocol === 'sse'`): keep the method dropdown (SSE supports GET, POST, etc.); replace the Send button with `<ConnectionControls tabId={activeTabId} onConnect={() => {/* TODO FEAT-007 */}} onDisconnect={() => {/* TODO FEAT-007 */}} />`.

  **Request section tabs**:
  - Replace the hard-coded `REQUEST_TABS` constant (currently `['Params', 'Headers', 'Body', 'Validation'] as const`) with a derived value per render:
    ```ts
    const visibleRequestTabs = getRequestTabsForProtocol(activeTab.protocol ?? 'http');
    ```
  - Render only `visibleRequestTabs` in the request tab bar.
  - The `setActiveRequestSection` logic and tab content rendering remain unchanged — `Validation` simply won't appear as a clickable tab for WS/SSE, so users can never activate it manually. (Store-level safety is already ensured by `updateProtocol` resetting the active section.)

  **Output / response area**:
  - Replace the response section block with a protocol branch:
    - `protocol === 'http'` (or undefined): render the existing response section exactly as-is (this branch is a 100% no-op change for HTTP).
    - `protocol === 'ws'`: render `<WsOutputArea tabId={activeTabId} />`.
    - `protocol === 'sse'`: render `<SseOutputArea tabId={activeTabId} />`.
  - The HTTP path retains the full existing response tabs, metadata bar, error view, etc. with no modifications.

- [ ] **Unit Tests**: Because `RequestEditor` is a platform-specific component (lives outside `packages/core`), full component tests are deferred. Verify correctness via:
  - Manual smoke test: new HTTP tab shows existing UI unmodified.
  - Manual smoke test: switching to WS hides method dropdown, shows ConnectionControls, shows only Params/Headers tabs, and renders the WsOutputArea placeholder.
  - Manual smoke test: switching to SSE keeps method dropdown, shows ConnectionControls, shows Params/Headers/Body tabs (no Validation), and renders the SseOutputArea placeholder.
  - Automated TypeScript check: `pnpm --filter @wave-client/vscode build` and `pnpm --filter @wave-client/web build` complete without errors.

- [ ] **Documentation Update**: Update the section comment blocks inside `RequestEditor.tsx` (both files) to note the protocol-conditional rendering branches.

- **Verify**: All protocol branches render without TypeScript errors. HTTP path unchanged. WS/SSE paths show correct controls and output areas.

---

## 6. Cross-Cutting Concerns

- **Auth**: `ProtocolSelector` does not change auth behaviour. Auth is applied at connection time by the adapter (FEAT-008/009). `ConnectionControls` does not touch auth; it only signals intent (connect/disconnect) via callbacks.
- **Error Handling**: `ConnectionControls` reads `ConnectionStatus = 'error'` from realtime state and shows the error dot/label. No adapter calls are made in this feature — there is nothing to fail.
- **Logging**: No new logging required. Connection lifecycle logging is in scope for FEAT-008/009 (adapter implementations).
- **Security**: `ProtocolSelector` only reads `tab.protocol` and calls `updateProtocol`. No external URLs, no auth tokens, no I/O. No security implications.
